steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence describes a gap in the rock record where material is missing from the stratigraphic sequence. The second sentence then explains those missing years: time “did not stand still,” and the material was removed by geological processes. The key signal is this phrase “did not stand still” — it clarifies an obvious fact about what happened during the gap.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: ”Of course” is the correct transition because it marks the second sentence as an obvious clarification: of course time didn't stand still; of course geological processes removed the material. The other choices misfit: “In particular” introduces a specific example; “On the contrary” implies an unstated contradiction; “As a result” reverses causation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
